ocr.text: In the analysis of costs associated with healthcare services at SEGMA hospitals and a University hospital, the table presents
various metrics across multiple categories. For the category "Invoice paid," there were 84 respondents at SEGMA hospitals
reporting a mean of $0 with a minimum and maximum of $0/$0, while the University hospital had 16 respondents with a mean
of $138 (95% CI: 98-178) and a minimum/maximum of $0*/$304. In the category "Pharmaceutical products and consumables,"
SEGMA hospitals had 82 respondents with a mean of $85 (95% CI: 76-96) and a minimum/maximum of $0/$228, compared to
15 respondents at the University hospital reporting a mean of $74 (51-98) with a minimum/maximum of $13/$158.
SEGMA hospitals  University hospital
Nr respondents  Mean(95% CI)  Minimum/Maximum  Nr respondents  Mean(95% CI)  Minimum/Maximum
Pharmaceutical products and consumables  82  $85 (76-96)  $0/$228  15  $74 (51-98)  $13/$158
Transport cost 1st journey  84  $6 (4-8)  $0/$63  16  $16 (7-24)  $0/$44
Tips and unofficial payments  74  $11 (7-14)  $0/$51  16  $3 (0-7)  $0/$19
For "Transport cost 1st journey," SEGMA hospitals recorded 84 respondents with a mean of $6 (4-8) and a minimum/maximum
of $0/$63, while the University hospital had 16 respondents reporting a mean of $16 (7-24) with a minimum/maximum of
$0/$44. In the "Transport cost (after 1st journey)" category, SEGMA hospitals had 84 respondents showing a mean of $46 (40-
52) and a minimum/maximum of $0/$121, while the University hospital had 16 respondents with a mean of $52 (28-78) and a
minimum/maximum of $5/$162. The total transport cost for SEGMA hospitals, with 84 respondents, was a mean of $52 (45-59)
and a minimum/maximum of $0/$163, compared to the University hospital's 16 respondents reporting a mean of $68 (42-94)
and a minimum/maximum of $5/$175. Regarding "Tips and unofficial payments," SEGMA hospitals had 74 respondents with a
mean of $11 (7-14) and a minimum/maximum of $0/$51, while the University hospital had 16 respondents reporting a mean of
$3 (0-7) and a minimum/maximum of $0/$19. "Cost of extra food" showed SEGMA hospitals with 84 respondents reporting a
mean of $16 (11-20) and a minimum/maximum of $0/$127, while the University hospital had 16 respondents with a mean of $5
(0-10) and a minimum/maximum of $0/$32. The opportunity cost was assessed with 83 respondents at SEGMA hospitals
reporting a mean of $8 (5-11) and a minimum/maximum of $0/$63, versus 15 respondents at the University hospital with a
mean of $7 (0-15) and a minimum/maximum of $0/$38. Finally, the "Global Cost of Caesarean" indicated 84 respondents at
SEGMA hospitals with a mean of $169 (153-185), a minimum of $26, and a maximum of $376, while the University hospital
had 16 respondents reporting a mean of $291 (224-359), with a minimum of $138 and a maximum of $616.
